behaviors.description: Branch staleness detection correctly identifies branches with commits not in main, older than the configured age threshold, and no open PR.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: detection must combine three signals ("commits not in main" + age threshold + "no open PR"). A Unit test of one predicate would miss a real failure where commit-age and PR-state modules use different branch keys/time units, causing stale branches to be misclassified. Rejected System because this behavior text does not require live repo/API execution; in-process git/PR adapters can reproduce this boundary failure.
behaviors.test_description: Wire the staleness detector to fake git-history and fake PR-state providers plus real config parsing; assert classification over mixed fixtures where only the full conjunction should produce stale.

behaviors.description: Session-start setup emits a stale-work warning with actionable branch context when the current branch is stale.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "Session-start setup emits ... when the current branch is stale" is a startup-orchestration handoff (bootstrap -> detector result -> warning renderer). A Unit test of just formatter logic would miss a real failure where startup wiring never invokes warning emission or drops branch context fields. Rejected System because behavior does not require real OS/subprocess/network; bootstrap can be invoked in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Run session bootstrap with injected stale branch state and capture emitted notices; verify one warning is produced and includes branch name/age/next action fields.

behaviors.description: A local status-classifier function maps computed branch state to labels (`clean`, `stale-no-pr`) deterministically.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly scopes to "A local status-classifier function" and "deterministically," which is a single-function mapping boundary. Higher levels are disqualified by text: no module handoff, no real infra, no model dependency, no multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Pure function table test over branch-state inputs asserting exact label outputs and deterministic repeatability.

behaviors.description: `/kaizen-wip` against live repo and PR state distinguishes "clean" from "has commits but no PR (STALE)" in rendered output.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "against live repo and PR state" requires real git/repo state and real PR-source behavior. An Integration test with mocked repo/PR clients can pass while real command/API interactions fail (e.g., auth/branch resolution/real state fetch differences), hiding the exact failure this behavior targets. Rejected Agentic because no AI/ML output quality is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Execute `/kaizen-wip` in a real repo context connected to real PR state source and assert rendered status flips between clean and STALE for prepared branch scenarios.

behaviors.description: Branches with open or merged PRs are not flagged as stale-no-PR false positives.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "open or merged PRs are not flagged" requires correct handoff between PR-state interpretation and classifier exclusion logic. A Unit test of classifier alone would miss a real failure where PR adapter maps merged/open states incorrectly into detector inputs. Rejected System because text does not require live PR API behavior; state-transition fixtures across open/merged can expose this boundary failure in-process.
behaviors.test_description: Integrate detector with PR-state adapter fixtures for open, merged, closed-unmerged, and none; assert only true no-PR branches can reach stale-no-pr.

behaviors.description: Staleness detection remains correct across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change but branch state should no longer be classified as unPRed stale work.
behaviors.justification: Escalation phrase: "across rebases or squash merges where commit hashes change" is real VCS-history behavior at git/OS boundary. Lower Integration with synthetic commit lists can miss graph/topology nuances after rewrite operations, causing stale classification to remain incorrect in real repos. Rejected Agentic because no model judgment is involved.
behaviors.test_description: Create real git histories that undergo rebase and squash-merge flows, then run staleness detection and assert branch is no longer marked stale-no-pr after history rewrite/merge completion.
